messages.content:
You are an expert at named entity recognition. Given an input text, extract all named entities along with their types and surrounding context.
Do not extract nested entities, only the outermost ones.

The possible labels for named entities are:
PER: names of people (different languages, nicknames, usernames, fictional characters, titles with names, etc.)
LOC: names of cities, countries, landmarks, geographical features, addresses, etc.
ORG: names of companies, institutions, agencies, teams, etc.
MISC: everything else that does not fit into the previous categories, but can still be considered a named entity (events, works of art, nationalities, religions, etc.)

Output format:
Return a JSON array of objects:
[
    { "entity": "ENTITY", "label": "ENTITY_LABEL", "context": "SURROUNDING_CONTEXT" },
    ...
]

Rules:
- "entity" must exactly match the original substring from the input text.
- "label" must be one of the specified entity labels.
- "context" must be a short snippet (5-7 words max) from the input text that contains the entity and a few neighboring words.
- If the entity is at the beginning or end of the text, use only the available neighboring words.
- If there are no named entities, output an empty JSON array [].

Example:
Input text: "Barack Obama was born in Hawaii."
Output:
[
    { "entity": "Barack Obama", "label": "PER", "context": "Barack Obama was born" },
    { "entity": "Hawaii", "label": "LOC", "context": "born in Hawaii." }
]

IMPORTANT: Only output the JSON array. DO NOT add any explanations.

Charles IV (German: Karl IV.; Czech: Karel IV.; Latin: Carolus IV; 14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1]), also known as Charles of Luxembourg, born Wenceslaus (German: Wenzel, Czech: Václav),[2] was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until his death in 1378. He was elected King of Germany (King of the Romans) in 1346 and became King of Bohemia (as Charles I) that same year. He was a member of the House of Luxembourg from his father's side and the Bohemian House of Přemyslid from his mother's side; he emphasized the latter due to his lifelong affinity for the Bohemian side of his inheritance, and also because his direct ancestors in the Přemyslid line included two saints.[3][4]

He was the eldest son and heir of John of Bohemia, King of Bohemia and Count of Luxembourg, who died at the Battle of Crécy on 26 August 1346. His mother, Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia, was the sister of Wenceslaus III, King of Bohemia and Poland, the last of the male Přemyslid rulers of Bohemia. Charles inherited the County of Luxembourg from his father and was elected king of the Kingdom of Bohemia. On 2 September 1347, Charles was crowned King of Bohemia.

[
    { "entity": "Charles IV", "label": "PER", "context": "Charles IV (German: Karl IV.; Czech:" },
    { "entity": "Charles of Luxembourg", "label": "PER", "context": "also known as Charles of Luxembourg, born" },
    { "entity": "Wenceslaus", "label": "PER", "context": "born Wenceslaus (German: Wenzel, Czech:" },
    { "entity": "John of Bohemia", "label": "PER", "context": "heir of John of Bohemia" },
    { "entity": "Elizabeth", "label": "PER", "context": "His mother, Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia" },
    { "entity": "Wenceslaus III", "label": "PER", "context": "sister of Wenceslaus III, King of Bohemia" },
    { "entity": "Holy Roman Emperor", "label": "MISC", "context": "was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until" },
    { "entity": "King of Germany", "label": "MISC", "context": "He was elected King of Germany (King" },
    { "entity": "King of the Romans", "label": "MISC", "context": "King of Germany (King of the" },
    { "entity": "King of Bohemia", "label": "MISC", "context": "became King of Bohemia (as Charles" },
    { "entity": "Queen of Bohemia", "label": "MISC", "context": "Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia, was the" },
    { "entity": "House of Luxembourg", "label": "ORG", "context": "member of the House of Luxembourg from" },
    { "entity": "Bohemian House of Přemyslid", "label": "ORG", "context": "and the Bohemian House of Přemyslid from" },
    { "entity": "Battle of Crécy", "label": "LOC", "context": "died at the Battle of Crécy" },
    { "entity": "Poland", "label": "LOC", "context": "King of Bohemia and Poland, the" },
    { "entity": "County of Luxembourg", "label": "LOC", "context": "Charles inherited the County of Luxembourg from" },
    { "entity": "Kingdom of Bohemia", "label": "LOC", "context": "king of the Kingdom of Bohemia" },
    { "entity": "14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378", "label": "MISC", "context": "14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1])" },
    { "entity": "26 August 1346", "label": "MISC", "context": "Battle of Crécy on 26 August 1346" },
    { "entity": "2 September 1347", "label": "MISC", "context": "On 2 September 1347, Charles was crowned" }
]

We need to extract named entities: PER, LOC, ORG, MISC. Provide context snippet 5-7 words. Avoid nested. Let's parse.

Text: "Charles IV (German: Karl IV.; Czech: Karel IV.; Latin: Carolus IV; 14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1]), also known as Charles of Luxembourg, born Wenceslaus (German: Wenzel, Czech: Václav),[2] was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until his death in 1378. He was elected King of Germany (King of the Romans) in 1346 and became King of Bohemia (as Charles I) that same year. He was a member of the House of Luxembourg from his father's side and the Bohemian House of Přemyslid from his mother's side; he emphasized the latter due to his lifelong affinity for the Bohemian side of his inheritance, and also because his direct ancestors in the Přemyslid line included two saints.[3][4]

He was the eldest son and heir of John of Bohemia, King of Bohemia and Count of Luxembourg, who died at the Battle of Crécy on 26 August 1346. His mother, Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia, was the sister of Wenceslaus III, King of Bohemia and Poland, the last of the male Přemyslid rulers of Bohemia. Charles inherited the County of Luxembourg from his father and was elected king of the Kingdom of Bohemia. On 2 September 1347, Charles was crowned King of Bohemia."
